56.reason: [Error: The problem requires that values greater than or equal to the root go to the right child, but this condition uses '>' which excludes the case when num == p->data. This will cause equal values to not be inserted correctly, potentially losing nodes or causing incorrect tree structure.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  Should be else (or >=) to include equal values; duplicates are lost and p may remain at node.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Logical Error] Fails to handle duplicate values. The problem specifies that the right child should be >= root, so this should be `else` or `else if(num >= p->data)` to correctly insert equal values.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Logical error: The problem specifies that the right child's value should be greater than or equal to the root. This condition should be num >= p->data to correctly handle duplicate values.

88.reason: [Logical Error] Unnecessary memory allocation. `top` should be initialized to `NULL` to correctly represent an empty tree before the first insertion.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Logical Error] Unnecessary memory allocation. `top` should be initialized to `NULL` to correctly represent an empty tree before the first insertion.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Unnecessary allocation. top is immediately overwritten in insert when p is NULL, and its l/r fields remain uninitialized, risking undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Unnecessary allocation: 'top' is immediately reassigned to NULL via 'p = NULL;' and later in insert(), leading to memory leak and undefined behavior when accessing uninitialized 'top'.]
